data.content: Now I'll create the `.template.config/template.json` to turn this project into a reusable `dotnet new` template.

arguments.command: mkdir -p /tmp/sv-49eced6b19a444f7b681d97be3af75e6/test-output/MyTests/.template.config

data.reasoningText:
I need to create a .NET template from this project. To do that, I need to:

1. Create a `.template.config` directory inside the project
2. Create a `template.json` file with the template configuration
3. The template should replace the namespace "MyTests" with the project name using `sourceName`

Let me create the template configuration.

arguments.command: mkdir -p /tmp/sv-49eced6b19a444f7b681d97be3af75e6/test-output/MyTests/.template.config

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-49eced6b19a444f7b681d97be3af75e6/test-output/MyTests/.template.config/template.json b/tmp/sv-49eced6b19a444f7b681d97be3af75e6/test-output/MyTests/.template.config/template.json
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-49eced6b19a444f7b681d97be3af75e6/test-output/MyTests/.template.config/template.json
@@ -1,0 +1,15 @@
+{
+  "$schema": "http://json.schemastore.org/template",
+  "author": "Team",
+  "classifications": ["Test", "xUnit"],
+  "identity": "TeamTestProject",
+  "name": "Team Test Project",
+  "shortName": "team-test",
+  "tags": {
+    "language": "C#",
+    "type": "project"
+  },
+  "sourceName": "MyTests",
+  "preferNameDirectory": true
+}
+

=== .csproj ===
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net9.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.11.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.9.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2">
      <IncludeAssets>runtime; build; native; contentfiles; analyzers; buildtransitive</IncludeAssets>
      <PrivateAssets>all</PrivateAssets>
    </PackageReference>
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

=== UnitTest1.cs ===
using Xunit;

namespace SampleTests;
public class UnitTest1
{
    [Fact]
    public void Test1() { }
}

data.content:
Template is working correctly. The namespace was replaced from `MyTests` → `SampleTests` and the `.csproj` was renamed.

Here's what was created:

**File:** `test-output/MyTests/.template.config/template.json`

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Team Test Project |
| **Short name** | `team-test` |
| **Tags** | C#, Test, xUnit |
| **sourceName** | `MyTests` (replaced with the `-n` value on `dotnet new`) |

Your team can use it with:
```
dotnet new team-test -n MyNewTests
```
